finding_text: An MRA has been issued related to data loss prevention controls. Examiners found that controls over encryption of data at rest are not commensurate with the institution's risk profile and complexity.

Matters Requiring Immediate Attention (MRIA) issued. Examiners found significant weaknesses in data classification and handling, including failure to periodic access recertification. The Board must establish a remediation timeline within 30 days. Failure to remediate may result in formal enforcement action.

Examiners noted that the institution's information security program is generally satisfactory. Minor deficiencies were identified in identity and access management, which management has committed to address by next examination cycle.
An MRA has been issued related to logging and monitoring. Examiners found that controls over encryption of data at rest are not commensurate with the institution's risk profile and complexity.
Review of identity and access management found policies and procedures to be consistent with regulatory expectations. Routine observations were communicated to management informally.
Review of incident response capabilities found policies and procedures to be consistent with regulatory expectations. Routine observations were communicated to management informally.
